etymology.description: 不(不) (bù, "not") negates the action of 改(改) (gǎi, "change"). Combining them conveys the meaning of maintaining consistency or staying the same.
etymology.interpretation: 不(不) (bù, "not") negates the component it's paired with. 改(改) (gǎi, "change") indicates the idea of alteration or modification. Therefore, 不改 conveys the concept of stopping any change from occurring, reinforcing constancy or the decision to keep something as it is.

examples.chinese: 无论发生什么(無論發生什麼)，他的决定不改(他的決定不改)。
examples.pinyin: Wúlùn fāshēng shénme, tā de juédìng bù gǎi.
examples.english: No matter what happens, his decision does not change.

examples.chinese: 他对原则不改(他對原則不改)，始终如一(始終如一)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì yuánzé bù gǎi, shǐzhōngrúyī.
examples.english: He does not change his principles and remains consistent.

examples.chinese: 虽然环境变了(雖然環境變了)，她的态度不改(她的態度不改)。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán huánjìng biàn le, tā de tàidù bù gǎi.
examples.english: Although the environment has changed, her attitude does not change.
